---
title: "Call for abstracts"
---

[**Sandy beaches as threatened social-ecological systems: addressing local to global challenges through actionable pathways for change**]{.text-primary style="font-size: 1.4rem;"}

The theme for ISBS10 links back to the theme of the first symposium in 1983, ‘Sandy beaches as ecosystems’, and highlights the advances in our understanding of sandy shores over the past four decades. It also acknowledges the deteriorating state of beaches worldwide, and the task that lies ahead of us to work together at multiple scales to secure beaches as diverse and productive ecosystems for current and future generations to enjoy.

## Contribution formats

The first three days of the symposium will comprise talks and poster sessions. We will be offering contributions of different lengths to keep the programme dynamic – you will have the option to select your top two preferences when you submit your abstract. To advertise the posters, we will also have ‘lightning rounds’ of very short talks with no questions (if you have questions, you can ask the presenter during the poster sessions).

- 15 min talk + 5 min for questions

- 12 min talk + 3 min for questions

- 7 min talk + 3 min for questions

- Poster + 2 min talk

## Topics

**You can submit your abstract under one of the following topics**

- Biodiversity patterns (species, habitats, ecosystems)

- Ecological processes and ecosystem services

- Pressures and impacts

- Human dimensions and social-ecological systems

- Policy, conservation, and management (includes coastal engineering, nature-based solutions)

- Other

## Submission guidelines

The online abstract submission system has fields where you can add authors, their affiliations, and type (or paste) your abstract text. Note that the word limit for abstracts is **250 words**.

**The deadline for submission is 28 February 2027.** We are opening the call early to allow plenty of time for you to apply for conference funding (which often requires confirmation of a submitted abstract).

[Submit now]{.btn .btn-secondary target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
